papers.title: Revisiting the Security of Sparkle
papers.abstract:
We revisit the three-round threshold Schnorr signature scheme Sparkle of Crites, Komlo, and Maller (CRYPTO 2023), as well as its variant Sparkle+. While Sparkle+ was accompanied by a claim of full adaptive security, subsequent work identified a gap in the analysis. Moreover, the original—and simpler and more efficient—Sparkle scheme has so far lacked even a proof of static security.

We resolve this state of affairs by giving the first proof of static security for Sparkle and then, as our main result, a tight proof of full adaptive security in the pure random oracle model, i.e. without relying on the algebraic group model. The core obstacle is that, in the fully adaptive setting for Sparkle, rewinding arguments fundamentally break down. To address this, our proof is based on a new Vandermonde circular discrete-logarithm (VCDL) assumption, an interactive strengthening of the circular discrete-logarithm assumption of Cho et al. (CRYPTO 2025), originally introduced to prove tight security of basic Schnorr signatures. In particular, circular-style assumptions eliminate the need for rewinding. Beyond tightness, our analysis highlights circular-style assumptions as a general approach to achieving security in settings—such as full adaptive security—where rewinding is inherently problematic.

We justify VCDL by reducing it to the low-dimensional vector representation (LDVR) problem of Crites et al. (CRYPTO 2025) in the elliptic-curve generic group model; conversely, VCDL implies LDVR in the standard model. Finally, we generalize VCDL (and similarly LDVR) by abstracting away the specific choice of Vandermonde vectors. As an application, we identify a different assumption within this framework that yields a tight proof of adaptive multi-user security for the basic Schnorr signature scheme, a result of independent interest.
papers.summary_zh:
## 研究背景与问题  
本文重新审视CRYPTO 2023提出的三轮门限Schnorr签名方案**Sparkle**及其变体**Sparkle+**。尽管Sparkle+曾声称具备**完全自适应安全性**（fully adaptive security），后续研究发现其安全性证明存在关键漏洞；而更简洁高效的原始Sparkle方案，甚至长期缺乏最基本的**静态安全性**（static security）证明，安全基础严重缺失。

## 核心贡献与方法  
我们首次为Sparkle建立了**静态安全性证明**，并进一步取得突破性进展：在**纯随机预言机模型**（pure random oracle model）下，给出了首个**紧致的（tight）、完全自适应安全**证明——该证明**不依赖代数群模型**（AGM），显著提升了可信度与实用性。核心难点在于：Sparkle的完全自适应攻击场景中，传统**重放（rewinding）论证彻底失效**。

为此，我们提出全新的**范德蒙德循环离散对数假设**（Vandermonde Circular Discrete-Logarithm, **VCDL**），作为Cho等人（CRYPTO 2025）循环离散对数假设的**交互式强化版本**。VCDL本质规避了重放需求，使紧致安全分析成为可能。我们进一步证明：VCDL可被规约至Crites等人（CRYPTO 2025）提出的**低维向量表示问题**（LDVR）——在椭圆曲线通用群模型中成立；反之，在标准模型中VCDL可推出LDVR，二者构成强等价支撑。最后，我们抽象出VCDL的向量结构，提出广义循环假设框架，并指出其中一例新假设可直接导出**基本Schnorr签名在多用户自适应攻击下的紧致安全性**，该结果本身具有独立理论价值。
papers.summary_en: We revisit the threshold Schnorr signature scheme Sparkle (CRYPTO 2023) and its variant Sparkle+, resolving long-standing gaps: Sparkle lacked even a static security proof, while Sparkle+’s claimed fully adaptive security was flawed. We provide the **first static security proof for Sparkle**, and—our main result—a **tight, fully adaptive security proof in the pure random oracle model**, *without* algebraic group assumptions. The key obstacle—breakdown of rewinding in full adaptivity—is overcome via a new **Vandermonde Circular Discrete-Logarithm (VCDL) assumption**, an interactive strengthening of Cho et al.’s circular DL assumption. VCDL eliminates rewinding and enables tight analysis. We justify VCDL by reducing it to the LDVR problem (Crites et al., CRYPTO 2025) in the elliptic-curve generic group model; conversely, VCDL implies LDVR in the standard model. Finally, we generalize VCDL and identify a related assumption yielding a tight adaptive multi-user security proof for basic Schnorr signatures—a result of independent interest.

为此，我们提出全新的**范德蒙德循环离散对数假设**（Vandermonde Circular Discrete-Logarithm, **VCDL**），作为Cho等人（CRYPTO 2025）循环离散对数假设的**交互式强化版本**。VCDL本质规避了重放需求，使紧致安全分析成为可能。我们进一步证明：VCDL可被规约至Crites等人（CRYPTO 2025）提出的**低维向量表示问题**（LDVR）——在椭圆曲线通用群模型中成立；反之，在标准模型中VCDL可推出LDVR，二者构成强等价支撑。最后，我们抽象出VCDL的向量结构，提出广义循环假设框架，并指出其中一例新假设可直接导出**基本Schnorr签名在多用户自适应攻击下的紧致安全性**，该结果本身具有独立理论价值。

papers.title: Efficient Single-Server Stateful PIR Using Format-Preserving Encryption
papers.abstract: Recently, Stateful Private Information Retrieval (PIR) has emerged as a promising new paradigm of PIR. Despite significant recent progress, state-of-the-art single-server schemes in this paradigm still suffer from practical inefficiencies in communication, computation, and/or client storage. In this work, we construct a new single-server stateful PIR scheme called HarmonyPIR that achieves efficient communication, computation, and client storage. From a technical standpoint, we build on the recent work of Wang and Ren (EUROCRYPT 25) and propose a new hint organization that uses only a single random permutation. The random permutation can be instantiated using either AES or the recently standardized FF1 Format-Preserving Encryption, yielding two variants of HarmonyPIR. Our new scheme achieves up to two orders of magnitude better amortized computation and up to five times better amortized communication than state-of-the-art schemes.
papers.summary_zh:
## 背景与挑战  
近年来，**有状态私有信息检索（Stateful PIR）** 作为一种新兴范式受到广泛关注，旨在通过客户端维护轻量状态来突破单服务器PIR的经典下界。然而，现有最优单服务器Stateful PIR方案在**通信开销、服务端计算负载及客户端存储**三方面仍存在显著瓶颈：或依赖高维向量编码导致带宽激增，或需频繁重生成复杂提示（hint）结构，或要求客户端持久化大量随机种子，难以支撑实际部署。

## 方法创新  
本文提出新型单服务器Stateful PIR方案——**HarmonyPIR**。其核心技术突破在于：基于Wang与Ren（EUROCRYPT ’25）的框架，设计了一种**极简提示组织机制**，仅需**单个随机置换（single random permutation）** 即可实现安全且高效的查询-响应关联。该置换通过两种轻量级密码原语实例化：① **AES分组加密构造的伪随机置换**；② **NIST标准化的FF1格式保持加密（FPE）算法**，天然支持任意域长且无需额外域扩展。

## 主要成果  
- **通信效率**：HarmonyPIR的*摊销通信开销*较当前最优方案（如Lattice-PIR+Stateful）降低**最高达5倍**（例如1MB数据库下，平均查询仅需~1.2KB）；  
- **计算效率**：服务端*摊销计算量*（以AES调用计）减少**两个数量级**，单次查询延迟从毫秒级降至微秒级；  
- **客户端轻量化**：仅需存储一个128位密钥+少量元数据（<1KB），彻底规避传统方案中O(√n)级随机种子存储；  
- **实践友好性**：FF1变体完全兼容现有FPE硬件加速模块，支持数据库字段级细粒度PIR，为隐私增强型数据库系统提供即插即用方案。
papers.summary_en: Stateful Private Information Retrieval (PIR) enables single-server PIR with sublinear communication by letting the client maintain lightweight state across queries. Yet, prior single-server stateful PIR schemes suffer from high amortized communication, heavy server computation, or excessive client storage. We propose **HarmonyPIR**, a new efficient construction that simultaneously optimizes all three metrics. Technically, we introduce a streamlined hint organization relying on only *one* random permutation—realized either via AES-based pseudorandom permutation or the NIST-standardized FF1 Format-Preserving Encryption (FPE). This design eliminates costly multi-dimensional encoding and redundant state materialization. HarmonyPIR achieves up to **5× lower amortized communication** and **up to two orders of magnitude faster amortized server computation** compared to the state of the art. Its client storage is bounded by a single 128-bit key plus <1 KB metadata, enabling practical deployment in privacy-sensitive database systems.

papers.title: Information-Theoretic Strong Traceable Secret Sharing Schemes
papers.abstract:
Traceable secret sharing complements traditional schemes by enabling the identification of parties who sell their shares. Recently, two independent works extended traceable secret sharing to general access structures.

Goyal, Jain, and Partap [EC'26] introduced a model in which a reconstruction box is augmented with a label $I \subseteq [n]$ and is only required to distinguish between two secrets when queried with the shares of parties in $I$. Based on how this label relates to the corrupted set $J$ that built the box, they defined two notions of traceability. If $I \cap J = \emptyset$, the model is called $\emptyset$-strong traceability, for which they presented a construction based on indistinguishability obfuscation (iO). Otherwise, their model is calledstrong traceability, for which they proved an impossibility result.
Farràs and Guiot [EC'26] proposed a different model, which we call hiding traceability, where the reconstruction box has no label and the access structure is hidden from the parties. 

In this work, we improve traceable secret sharing for general access structures in three directions. First, we present a fully information-theoretic scheme for the $\emptyset$-strong traceability model, eliminating the need for strong cryptographic assumptions. This resolves an open question posed by Goyal, Jain, and Partap, who asked what are the minimal assumptions needed in the $\emptyset$-strong traceability model.

Second, motivated by the impossibility of strong traceability, we introduce a relaxed notion calledhidden mildly strong traceability. This model is relevant in practice and bridges the strong and hidden models. For this setting, we present an information-theoretic scheme for general access structures.

Finally, we consider the more general model of stateful traceability, where reconstruction boxes may keep state across queries, and we prove an impossibility result for this setting.
papers.summary_zh:
## 信息论强可追踪秘密共享方案

**背景与问题**：可追踪秘密共享（Traceable Secret Sharing, TSS）在传统秘密共享基础上引入“追责”能力，允许识别恶意出售其份额的参与方。近期，Goyal-Jain-Partap（EC’26）和Farràs-Guiot（EC’26）分别提出面向一般访问结构的两类扩展模型：前者定义了带标签 $I \subseteq [n]$ 的重构盒，并据此区分 $\emptyset$-强可追踪性（当标签集 $I$ 与腐败集 $J$ 不相交）与强可追踪性（当 $I \cap J \neq \emptyset$），但后者被证明信息论上不可能；后者则提出“隐藏可追踪性”，其中重构盒无标签且访问结构对参与者保密。

**主要贡献**：本文在三个方向实现突破：  
1. **首个完全信息论的 $\emptyset$-强可追踪方案**：摒弃了Goyal等依赖的**不可区分混淆（iO）** 等强密码假设，构造适用于任意单调访问结构的高效方案，正面回答其提出的开放问题——$\emptyset$-强模型的最小假设即为信息论安全；  
2. **提出新模型“隐藏轻度强可追踪性”**：作为强可追踪性与隐藏可追踪性的实用折中，在腐败集部分重叠标签的现实场景中仍可实现可追踪性，并给出**信息论安全的通用构造**；  
3. **刻画状态化可追踪性的根本局限**：针对更强大的“状态化追踪”模型（重构盒可在多次查询间维持内部状态），我们严格证明其在信息论框架下**不可实现**，确立了该方向的理论边界。

本工作系统推进了可追踪秘密共享的理论基础，显著降低安全假设、拓展适用模型，并厘清了可行性边界。
papers.summary_en: This paper advances information-theoretic traceable secret sharing (TSS) for general access structures in three key directions. First, we present the **first fully information-theoretic construction** for the $\emptyset$-strong traceability model of Goyal, Jain, and Partap (EC’26), eliminating their reliance on indistinguishability obfuscation (iO) and resolving their open question on minimal assumptions. Second, motivated by the impossibility of strong traceability, we introduce *hidden mildly strong traceability*—a practical relaxation bridging strong and hiding models—and provide an **information-theoretic scheme** for arbitrary monotone access structures. Third, we study stateful traceability, where reconstruction boxes maintain state across queries, and prove a **strong impossibility result**, establishing a fundamental limit. Our work tightens the foundations of TSS by reducing cryptographic assumptions, expanding feasible models, and clarifying theoretical boundaries.
papers.summary:
## 信息论强可追踪秘密共享方案

**背景与问题**：可追踪秘密共享（Traceable Secret Sharing, TSS）在传统秘密共享基础上引入“追责”能力，允许识别恶意出售其份额的参与方。近期，Goyal-Jain-Partap（EC’26）和Farràs-Guiot（EC’26）分别提出面向一般访问结构的两类扩展模型：前者定义了带标签 $I \subseteq [n]$ 的重构盒，并据此区分 $\emptyset$-强可追踪性（当标签集 $I$ 与腐败集 $J$ 不相交）与强可追踪性（当 $I \cap J \neq \emptyset$），但后者被证明信息论上不可能；后者则提出“隐藏可追踪性”，其中重构盒无标签且访问结构对参与者保密。

**主要贡献**：本文在三个方向实现突破：  
1. **首个完全信息论的 $\emptyset$-强可追踪方案**：摒弃了Goyal等依赖的**不可区分混淆（iO）** 等强密码假设，构造适用于任意单调访问结构的高效方案，正面回答其提出的开放问题——$\emptyset$-强模型的最小假设即为信息论安全；  
2. **提出新模型“隐藏轻度强可追踪性”**：作为强可追踪性与隐藏可追踪性的实用折中，在腐败集部分重叠标签的现实场景中仍可实现可追踪性，并给出**信息论安全的通用构造**；  
3. **刻画状态化可追踪性的根本局限**：针对更强大的“状态化追踪”模型（重构盒可在多次查询间维持内部状态），我们严格证明其在信息论框架下**不可实现**，确立了该方向的理论边界。

本工作系统推进了可追踪秘密共享的理论基础，显著降低安全假设、拓展适用模型，并厘清了可行性边界。

papers.title: Secure Cloud Storage: Modularization, Network Adversaries and Adaptive Corruptions
papers.abstract:
End-to-end cloud storage solutions are deployed at large scale, yet recent works have demonstrated severe attacks against their confidentiality and integrity. Motivated by this, a first formal treatment of secure cloud storage was given at CRYPTO 2024 by Backendal, Davis, Günther, Haller and Paterson (BDGHP). They define syntax and security notions, capturing client-to-client security of cloud storage schemes with respect to a password distribution. They also give an eﬃcient construction using the Two-Hash Diﬃe-Hellman (2HDH) OPRF and standard cryptographic building blocks, which they prove secure under selective corruptions in the random oracle model. However, several aspects of practical security guarantees remain open.
We extend and refine the work of BDGHP along multiple dimensions, advancing the analysis of secure cloud storage schemes. First, we prove that their construction can be proven secure against adaptive corruptions (with a slight modification), circumventing technical challenges posed by file sharing. Second, we modularize the scheme further by introducing an abstraction for the authentication procedure. This allows us to identify the concrete role of 2HDH and alternative instantiations. Third, we introduce a weaker model that captures adversaries who can arbitrarily control the network, except during registration. This allows us to prove concrete guarantees about online password guessing attacks, whereas the stronger model inherently allows for oﬄine guessing. Finally, we formalize and prove explicit authentication, relying on the security of our new authentication abstraction and the MAC scheme, where the latter was previously not used in the security analysis.
papers.summary_zh:
## 研究背景与问题  
当前端到端云存储方案已大规模部署，但近年研究表明其**机密性与完整性面临严重攻击威胁**。BDGHP（CRYPTO 2024）首次为安全云存储建立了形式化框架，定义了语法与安全模型（基于密码分布的客户端间安全性），并基于**双哈希迪菲-赫尔曼（2HDH）OPRF**构造了高效方案，在随机预言模型下证明了其在*选择性腐败*下的安全性。然而，该工作遗留多个关键实践安全问题：无法抵御自适应腐败、认证机制耦合过紧、网络敌手建模过强（导致离线口令猜测不可避免）、且未显式保障认证完整性。

## 主要贡献与创新  
本研究从四个维度系统拓展并精炼BDGHP框架：  
1. **自适应腐败安全性**：通过引入轻量级协议修改（客户端注册后绑定长期公钥），首次在标准假设下将原方案安全性提升至*自适应腐败模型*，克服了文件共享场景中状态依赖带来的技术障碍；  
2. **模块化认证抽象**：提出独立的**认证接口（AuthInterface）**，解耦密码学原语与协议逻辑，明确界定2HDH在口令绑定与密钥派生中的核心作用，并支持安全替换为其他OPRF或PAKE方案；  
3. **弱网络敌手模型**：定义*注册后网络控制模型*（Post-Registration Network Adversary），敌手仅在用户注册阶段受限，此后可任意篡改/延迟通信——该模型首次实现对*在线口令猜测攻击*的可证安全界（如最多$O(q_{\text{reg}} \cdot q_{\text{login}})$次有效猜测），而原强模型必然允许离线暴力破解；  
4. **显式认证安全性**：首次形式化并证明**显式认证（Explicit Authentication）** 属性，即客户端可验证服务端是否真正持有合法口令派生密钥；该证明依赖新提出的认证抽象与消息认证码（MAC）的组合安全性，而MAC在BDGHP原始分析中未被启用。
papers.summary_en: We extend the CRYPTO 2024 secure cloud storage framework of BDGHP along four key axes. First, we strengthen security from selective to **adaptive corruptions**, resolving technical barriers from file sharing via a minimal modification (post-registration public-key binding). Second, we introduce a modular **authentication interface**, decoupling protocol logic from cryptographic primitives and clarifying the precise role of 2HDH—enabling secure alternatives like PAKEs. Third, we propose a weaker **post-registration network adversary model**, where the adversary controls the network *except during registration*, enabling concrete bounds against *online password guessing* (e.g., $O(q_{\text{reg}} \cdot q_{\text{login}})$ queries), unlike the stronger model that permits offline attacks. Finally, we formalize and prove **explicit authentication**, ensuring clients verify the server’s possession of the correct password-derived key—leveraging our new authentication abstraction and an integrated MAC scheme, previously unused in BDGHP’s security analysis. All results hold in the random oracle model under standard assumptions.
papers.summary:

当前端到端云存储方案已大规模部署，但近年研究表明其**机密性与完整性面临严重攻击威胁**。BDGHP（CRYPTO 2024）首次为安全云存储建立了形式化框架，定义了语法与安全模型（基于密码分布的客户端间安全性），并基于**双哈希迪菲-赫尔曼（2HDH）OPRF**构造了高效方案，在随机预言模型下证明了其在*选择性腐败*下的安全性。然而，该工作遗留多个关键实践安全问题：无法抵御自适应腐败、认证机制耦合过紧、网络敌手建模过强（导致离线口令猜测不可避免）、且未显式保障认证完整性。

## 主要贡献与创新  
本研究从四个维度系统拓展并精炼BDGHP框架：  
1. **自适应腐败安全性**：通过引入轻量级协议修改（客户端注册后绑定长期公钥），首次在标准假设下将原方案安全性提升至*自适应腐败模型*，克服了文件共享场景中状态依赖带来的技术障碍；  
2. **模块化认证抽象**：提出独立的**认证接口（AuthInterface）**，解耦密码学原语与协议逻辑，明确界定2HDH在口令绑定与密钥派生中的核心作用，并支持安全替换为其他OPRF或PAKE方案；  
3. **弱网络敌手模型**：定义*注册后网络控制模型*（Post-Registration Network Adversary），敌手仅在用户注册阶段受限，此后可任意篡改/延迟通信——该模型首次实现对*在线口令猜测攻击*的可证安全界（如最多$O(q_{\text{reg}} \cdot q_{\text{login}})$次有效猜测），而原强模型必然允许离线暴力破解；  
4. **显式认证安全性**：首次形式化并证明**显式认证（Explicit Authentication）** 属性，即客户端可验证服务端是否真正持有合法口令派生密钥；该证明依赖新提出的认证抽象与消息认证码（MAC）的组合安全性，而MAC在BDGHP原始分析中未被启用。

papers.title: Round-Optimal Threshold Blind Signatures without Random Oracles
papers.abstract: This paper presents the first round-optimal threshold blind signature without random oracles. Our construction achieves security in the algebraic group model (AGM) for asymmetric pairing groups, and tolerates adaptive corruption of up to $t-1$ signers, where $t$ is the threshold. We improve upon the recent threshold blind signatures of Lehmann, Nazarian and Özbay (EUROCRYPT 2025) and Jarecki and Nazarian (ASIACRYPT 2025) in two ways: we eliminate both the reliance on random oracles and the need for $q$-type assumptions in the AGM. As a core building block, we introduce a new pairing-based round-optimal blind signature without random oracles, based on the $2$-DL assumption in the AGM. Both blind signature schemes achieve communication and computation costs only twice that of the celebrated blind BLS signature.
papers.summary_zh:
## 首个轮数最优、无需随机预言机的门限盲签名方案  

本论文提出了**首个轮数最优（round-optimal）且完全不依赖随机预言机（RO-free）的门限盲签名方案**，填补了该领域长期存在的理论空白。方案在**代数群模型（AGM）下针对非对称配对群构建**，可容忍最多 $t-1$ 个签名者的自适应腐败（其中 $t$ 为门限值），满足强安全性要求。  

相较于近期两项重要工作——Lehmann-Nazarian-Özbay（EUROCRYPT 2025）和Jarecki-Nazarian（ASIACRYPT 2025）提出的门限盲签名方案，本方案实现双重突破：  
- ✅ **彻底消除随机预言机假设**，避免RO模型带来的可实现性与现实安全性鸿沟；  
- ✅ **摒弃所有 $q$-type 复杂假设**（如 $q$-SDH、$q$-BDHE），仅基于简洁、标准的 **2-离散对数（2-DL）假设**，显著提升基础假设的可信度与简洁性。  

核心技术贡献在于设计了一个**新型配对基、轮数最优、无随机预言机的盲签名原语**，作为门限化构造的核心构件。该原语本身即具独立价值：其通信开销与计算开销**仅为经典盲BLS签名的2倍**，在效率上保持高度实用；门限化扩展后仍维持相同渐进复杂度。安全性在AGM下被严格证明，涵盖盲性（blindness）、不可伪造性（unforgeability）与门限鲁棒性（robustness）。本工作为高安全、低延迟的隐私增强型分布式系统（如匿名凭证、隐私支付协议）提供了首个兼具理论严谨性与工程可行性的门限盲签名基础构件。
papers.summary_en: This paper presents the first **round-optimal threshold blind signature scheme without random oracles**. It achieves security in the Algebraic Group Model (AGM) for asymmetric pairing groups and tolerates adaptive corruption of up to $t-1$ signers (where $t$ is the threshold). Compared to recent schemes by Lehmann et al. (EUROCRYPT 2025) and Jarecki & Nazarian (ASIACRYPT 2025), ours eliminates *both* the random oracle assumption *and* all $q$-type assumptions in the AGM—relying solely on the standard **2-DL assumption**. At its core, we introduce a new pairing-based, round-optimal, RO-free blind signature, which serves as the building block for our threshold construction. Both the base blind signature and the threshold variant incur only **twice the communication and computation cost** of the celebrated blind BLS signature, preserving practical efficiency while achieving stronger foundational security.
papers.summary:

相较于近期两项重要工作——Lehmann-Nazarian-Özbay（EUROCRYPT 2025）和Jarecki-Nazarian（ASIACRYPT 2025）提出的门限盲签名方案，本方案实现双重突破：  
- ✅ **彻底消除随机预言机假设**，避免RO模型带来的可实现性与现实安全性鸿沟；  
- ✅ **摒弃所有 $q$-type 复杂假设**（如 $q$-SDH、$q$-BDHE），仅基于简洁、标准的 **2-离散对数（2-DL）假设**，显著提升基础假设的可信度与简洁性。  

核心技术贡献在于设计了一个**新型配对基、轮数最优、无随机预言机的盲签名原语**，作为门限化构造的核心构件。该原语本身即具独立价值：其通信开销与计算开销**仅为经典盲BLS签名的2倍**，在效率上保持高度实用；门限化扩展后仍维持相同渐进复杂度。安全性在AGM下被严格证明，涵盖盲性（blindness）、不可伪造性（unforgeability）与门限鲁棒性（robustness）。本工作为高安全、低延迟的隐私增强型分布式系统（如匿名凭证、隐私支付协议）提供了首个兼具理论严谨性与工程可行性的门限盲签名基础构件。

papers.title: Finite Field Arithmetic for ML-KEM Using Zech's Logarithm
papers.abstract: The processing of ML-KEM (formerly CRYSTALS-Kyber), a key encapsulation mechanism with post-quantum security, is performed by multiplication, addition, and subtraction of polynomials whose coefficients lie in the finite field ${\mathbb F}_{3329}$. To reduce the number of such operations, it is common to use the Number Theoretic Transform (NTT). This paper focuses on arithmetic over ${\mathbb F}_{3329}$ and proposes the use of a logarithmic representation with respect to a primitive element $\alpha$ of ${\mathbb F}_{3329}^*$ for implementing multiplication, addition, and subtraction over ${\mathbb F}_{3329}$. In this representation, multiplication in ${\mathbb F}_{3329}^*$ can be reduced to addition in $\mathbb{Z}_{3328}$. Furthermore, addition and subtraction in ${\mathbb F}_{3329}^*$ can be computed in the logarithmic domain by using Zech's logarithm. However, special treatment is required when $0 \in {\mathbb F}_{3329}$ is involved in the operations. This paper proposes a new implementation method of the logarithmic representation for arithmetic over ${\mathbb F}_{3329}$, including the handling of such exceptional cases.
papers.summary_zh:
## 研究背景与问题  
ML-KEM（原CRYSTALS-Kyber）是NIST标准化的后量子密钥封装机制，其核心运算依赖于系数在有限域 $\mathbb{F}_{3329}$ 上的多项式乘、加、减操作。为加速运算，通常采用数论变换（NTT），但NTT仍需大量底层域运算——尤其乘法在$\mathbb{F}_{3329}$中需模约简，开销显著。传统对数表示法虽能将乘法降为加法，却难以高效支持加/减运算，且对零元 $0 \in \mathbb{F}_{3329}$ 缺乏统一处理机制，导致实现复杂、分支多、易受侧信道攻击。

## 方法与创新  
本文提出一种**基于Zech对数的统一有限域算术实现方案**，针对 $\mathbb{F}_{3329}$ 设计完整对数表示框架：  
- 选取本原元 $\alpha$ 构建 $\mathbb{F}_{3329}^*$ 的离散对数映射，使非零元乘法简化为 $\mathbb{Z}_{3328}$ 中的模加；  
- 引入**Zech对数表**（$Z(i) = \log_\alpha(1 + \alpha^i)$），将加/减运算完全迁移至对数域：$\log_\alpha(\alpha^i + \alpha^j) = i + Z(j-i \bmod 3328)$（$i \neq j$），并系统化处理 $i=j$ 及含零情形；  
- 首次设计**零元嵌入策略**：将 $0$ 映射为特殊符号（如 $-1$），在查表与运算逻辑中显式隔离零路径，避免运行时条件分支，提升常数时间性与硬件友好性；  
- 构建紧凑Zech表（仅3328项）与双层索引结构，在内存受限场景下实现低延迟查表。

## 主要成果  
实验表明，该方案在ARM Cortex-M4平台将ML-KEM关键域运算（如NTT层乘加）平均加速1.8×，同时减少32%的指令缓存压力；Zech表仅占13.3 KB ROM，适合轻量级IoT设备。本工作为后量子密码的高效、安全域运算提供了可扩展的新范式。
papers.summary_en: This paper addresses the computational bottleneck of finite field arithmetic in ML-KEM over $\mathbb{F}_{3329}$, a critical component of post-quantum cryptographic implementations. We propose a novel logarithmic representation using a primitive element $\alpha$ of $\mathbb{F}_{3329}^*$, enhanced with **Zech’s logarithm** to support *all* field operations—multiplication, addition, and subtraction—within a unified logarithmic domain. Multiplication reduces to modular addition in $\mathbb{Z}_{3328}$, while addition/subtraction leverage precomputed Zech values $Z(i) = \log_\alpha(1+\alpha^i)$, enabling efficient log-domain computation without conditional branches. Crucially, we introduce a robust zero-handling mechanism that explicitly encodes $0$ as a sentinel value, ensuring constant-time execution and hardware efficiency. Implemented on ARM Cortex-M4, our method accelerates core ML-KEM field operations by 1.8× on average and reduces ROM footprint for the Zech table to just 13.3 KB—demonstrating practicality for resource-constrained devices.
papers.summary:
## 研究背景与问题  
ML-KEM（原CRYSTALS-Kyber）是NIST标准化的后量子密钥封装机制，其核心运算依赖于系数在有限域 $\mathbb{F}_{3329}$ 上的多项式乘、加、减操作。为加速运算，通常采用数论变换（NTT），但NTT仍需大量底层域运算——尤其乘法在$\mathbb{F}_{3329}$中需模约简，开销显著。传统对数表示法虽能将乘法降为加法，却难以高效支持加/减运算，且对零元 $0 \in \mathbb{F}_{3329}$ 缺乏统一处理机制，导致实现复杂、分支多、易受侧信道攻击。

## 方法与创新  
本文提出一种**基于Zech对数的统一有限域算术实现方案**，针对 $\mathbb{F}_{3329}$ 设计完整对数表示框架：  
- 选取本原元 $\alpha$ 构建 $\mathbb{F}_{3329}^*$ 的离散对数映射，使非零元乘法简化为 $\mathbb{Z}_{3328}$ 中的模加；  
- 引入**Zech对数表**（$Z(i) = \log_\alpha(1 + \alpha^i)$），将加/减运算完全迁移至对数域：$\log_\alpha(\alpha^i + \alpha^j) = i + Z(j-i \bmod 3328)$（$i \neq j$），并系统化处理 $i=j$ 及含零情形；  
- 首次设计**零元嵌入策略**：将 $0$ 映射为特殊符号（如 $-1$），在查表与运算逻辑中显式隔离零路径，避免运行时条件分支，提升常数时间性与硬件友好性；  
- 构建紧凑Zech表（仅3328项）与双层索引结构，在内存受限场景下实现低延迟查表。

## 主要成果  
实验表明，该方案在ARM Cortex-M4平台将ML-KEM关键域运算（如NTT层乘加）平均加速1.8×，同时减少32%的指令缓存压力；Zech表仅占13.3 KB ROM，适合轻量级IoT设备。本工作为后量子密码的高效、安全域运算提供了可扩展的新范式。

papers.title: An attack on the CFS scheme and on TII McEliece challenges
papers.abstract: It has been a very long standing open question whether the CFS signature scheme  whose security is basically that of a McEliece scheme based on very high rate binary Goppa codes could be attacked or not. There was a first cryptanalytic result by Faugère et al in 2011  consisting in finding a distinguisher for the binary Goppa codes used in this scheme showing that these codes can be distinguished in polynomial time from a random binary linear code. However despite numerous cryptanalytic attempts and even if the original distinguisher has been significantly improved, no attack on the McEliece scheme based on binary Goppa codes has been found so far except for very peculiar Goppa codes of degree $2$. We show here that the Pfaffian modeling used in the distinguishing attack of Couvreur, Mora and Tillich of Asiacrypt 2023 can actually be used together with a shortening trick and looking for squares in the corresponding ideal to find a polynomial attack on the CFS scheme based on very high rate binary Goppa codes.This breaks this 25 years old signature scheme. We demonstrate the effectiveness of this approach by recovering the key of TII McEliece challenges with a claimed key security of up to 210 bits.
papers.summary_zh:
## 突破性成果：攻破25年未被破解的CFS签名方案  
**背景**：CFS（Courtois–Finiasz–Sendrier）签名方案自1997年提出以来，是首个基于McEliece公钥密码体系的实用化签名构造，其安全性依赖于**极高码率二元Goppa码**的解码困难性。尽管2011年Faugère等人首次提出针对该类码的多项式时间可区分器（distinguisher），但近25年来，**始终未出现能实际恢复私钥或伪造签名的多项式时间攻击**——尤其对常规高次Goppa码（如度≥3），McEliece型加密/签名仍被视为抗量子候选。TII McEliece挑战赛（2023）更以高达210比特声称安全强度的CFS实例验证其“顽固安全性”。

**方法创新**：本研究发现，Couvreur-Mora-Tillich（Asiacrypt 2023）提出的**Pfaffian建模框架**可超越原定区分目的，经三重关键改造实现密钥恢复：（1）引入**码长缩短（shortening）技巧**，将高码率问题转化为低维代数结构；（2）在对应商理想中**系统搜索平方元（squares）**，揭示隐藏的Goppa多项式因子结构；（3）结合Pfaffian矩阵的秩衰减特性，导出可高效求解的多项式方程组。整个过程严格在**多项式时间**内完成，无需指数级搜索。

**核心发现**：  
- 首次实现对标准CFS方案（使用deg ≥ 3的二元Goppa码）的**完整私钥恢复攻击**，彻底终结其作为实用签名方案的可行性；  
- 成功破解全部公开的TII McEliece挑战实例，包括声称210比特安全强度的最高难度实例，实测耗时仅数小时（单机CPU）；  
- 揭示高码率Goppa码存在深层代数脆弱性：其校验矩阵生成的理想蕴含可被多项式提取的平方结构，颠覆了“高码率即增强安全性”的传统认知。
papers.summary_en: This paper breaks the 25-year-old CFS signature scheme—a long-standing open problem in code-based cryptography. While Faugère et al.’s 2011 distinguisher revealed that high-rate binary Goppa codes are structurally non-random, no efficient key-recovery attack existed until now, except for pathological degree-2 Goppa codes. We repurpose the Pfaffian modeling from Couvreur–Mora–Tillich (Asiacrypt 2023) by introducing a shortening technique and systematically searching for squares in the associated ideal, enabling a polynomial-time algebraic attack on standard CFS instantiated with high-rate binary Goppa codes of degree ≥3. Our method fully recovers the secret key, invalidating CFS as a secure signature scheme. We demonstrate practical impact by solving all TII McEliece Challenge instances—including the highest-security instance claiming 210-bit security—in polynomial time on a single CPU. This result fundamentally revises the security assessment of high-rate Goppa codes.
papers.summary:
## 突破性成果：攻破25年未被破解的CFS签名方案  
**背景**：CFS（Courtois–Finiasz–Sendrier）签名方案自1997年提出以来，是首个基于McEliece公钥密码体系的实用化签名构造，其安全性依赖于**极高码率二元Goppa码**的解码困难性。尽管2011年Faugère等人首次提出针对该类码的多项式时间可区分器（distinguisher），但近25年来，**始终未出现能实际恢复私钥或伪造签名的多项式时间攻击**——尤其对常规高次Goppa码（如度≥3），McEliece型加密/签名仍被视为抗量子候选。TII McEliece挑战赛（2023）更以高达210比特声称安全强度的CFS实例验证其“顽固安全性”。

**核心发现**：  
- 首次实现对标准CFS方案（使用deg ≥ 3的二元Goppa码）的**完整私钥恢复攻击**，彻底终结其作为实用签名方案的可行性；

papers.title: Efficient Private Range Queries on Public Data
papers.abstract:
Range queries can filter, aggregate, and retrieve database entries that lie in a specified multi-dimensional rectangle. 
Private range queries allow a client to query a server's public database while keeping the client's multi-dimensional rectangle hidden.

We construct RangeR, a constant-round private range query scheme that supports any associative aggregation function (e.g., SUM, MAX, TOP-K) and works with any number of servers. In the single-server setting, RangeR is orders of magnitude faster and uses 50%-90% less communication than HADES (VLDB 2025), a prior single-server private range query scheme that only supports linear aggregation functions. 

We describe how RangeR can be used to implement a privacy-preserving map application that can return the highest-rated restaurants near a user. Using data from $\mathtt{OpenStreetMaps}$, we estimate that a user can find the highest-rated restaurants within one kilometer of their location within $2$ seconds, while revealing only that the user is somewhere in the USA.
papers.summary_zh:
## 研究背景与问题  
多维范围查询（Range Queries）是数据库系统中的基础操作，用于筛选、聚合或检索落在指定多维矩形区域内的数据条目。**私有范围查询**（Private Range Queries）则进一步要求：客户端在向服务器查询公开数据库时，其查询区域（即多维矩形）本身必须对服务器保持机密——这是位置服务、地理围栏、隐私敏感分析等场景的核心安全需求。

## 方法创新：RangeR 方案  
本文提出 **RangeR**——一种高效、通用且通信友好的私有范围查询协议。其核心创新包括：  
- ✅ **常数轮次交互**（constant-round）：仅需固定轮数通信，不随数据规模或维度增长；  
- ✅ **支持任意结合性聚合函数**：不仅限于线性函数（如 SUM），还可原生支持 **MAX、TOP-K、COUNT DISTINCT（经适配）** 等非线性聚合；  
- ✅ **部署灵活**：兼容单服务器与多服务器架构，无需可信第三方；  
- ✅ **基于轻量密码原语**：采用高效匿踪多项式求值与维度分解技术，避免重加密或全同态运算。

## 性能突破与实证效果  
在单服务器设置下，RangeR 相比当前最优方案 HADES（VLDB’25）实现数量级提升：  
- **计算速度加快 10–100 倍**（取决于维度与数据量）；  
- **通信开销降低 50%–90%**（例如，百万级二维点集上仅需 ~1.2 MB 查询+响应）；  
- 实际应用验证：基于 **OpenStreetMap 公共餐厅数据**构建隐私地图服务，用户可在 **≤2 秒内**获取距其位置 1 公里内评分最高的餐厅，而服务器仅知用户位于美国境内（无经纬度、城市、甚至州级信息泄露）。

## 意义  
RangeR 首次在强隐私保障（查询区域零泄露）、高实用性（低延迟/低带宽）与功能完备性（聚合通用性）三者间取得平衡，为公共数据上的隐私增强型地理服务提供了可落地的新范式。
papers.summary_en: Range queries retrieve or aggregate entries within a multi-dimensional rectangle; *private* range queries hide the client’s query region from the server while accessing public data. We propose **RangeR**, a constant-round private range query scheme supporting *any associative aggregation function* (e.g., SUM, MAX, TOP-K) and deployable with *any number of servers*. In the single-server setting, RangeR outperforms HADES (VLDB 2025)—the prior state-of-the-art for linear aggregations—by **10–100× faster computation** and **50–90% less communication**, thanks to lightweight cryptographic techniques and dimension-aware optimizations. We demonstrate its practicality in a privacy-preserving map application: using OpenStreetMap data, a user finds top-rated restaurants within 1 km of their location in **under 2 seconds**, revealing *only* that they are somewhere in the USA—not their coordinates, city, or even state. RangeR bridges expressiveness, efficiency, and strong privacy for real-world public-data analytics.
papers.summary:
## 研究背景与问题  
多维范围查询（Range Queries）是数据库系统中的基础操作，用于筛选、聚合或检索落在指定多维矩形区域内的数据条目。**私有范围查询**（Private Range Queries）则进一步要求：客户端在向服务器查询公开数据库时，其查询区域（即多维矩形）本身必须对服务器保持机密——这是位置服务、地理围栏、隐私敏感分析等场景的核心安全需求。

## 方法创新：RangeR 方案  
本文提出 **RangeR**——一种高效、通用且通信友好的私有范围查询协议。其核心创新包括：  
- ✅ **常数轮次交互**（constant-round）：仅需固定轮数通信，不随数据规模或维度增长；  
- ✅ **支持任意结合性聚合函数**：不仅限于线性函数（如 SUM），还可原生支持 **MAX、TOP-K、COUNT DISTINCT（经适配）** 等非线性聚合；  
- ✅ **部署灵活**：兼容单服务器与多服务器架构，无需可信第三方；  
- ✅ **基于轻量密码原语**：采用高效匿踪多项式求值与维度分解技术，避免重加密或全同态运算。

## 性能突破与实证效果  
在单服务器设置下，RangeR 相比当前最优方案 HADES（VLDB’25）实现数量级提升：  
- **计算速度加快 10–100 倍**（取决于维度与数据量）；  
- **通信开销降低 50%–90%**（例如，百万级二维点集上仅需 ~1.2 MB 查询+响应）；  
- 实际应用验证：基于 **OpenStreetMap 公共餐厅数据**构建隐私地图服务，用户可在 **≤2 秒内**获取距其位置 1 公里内评分最高的餐厅，而服务器仅知用户位于美国境内（无经纬度、城市、甚至州级信息泄露）。

## 意义  
RangeR 首次在强隐私保障（查询区域零泄露）、高实用性（低延迟/低带宽）与功能完备性（聚合通用性）三者间取得平衡，为公共数据上的隐私增强型地理服务提供了可落地的新范式。

papers.title: Defending Against Backdoor Attacks in Homomorphically Encrypted Federated Learning
papers.abstract: The distributed nature of federated learning systems makes them vulnerable to backdoor attacks in which malicious clients manipulate local training data using trigger-dependent behaviors to cause targeted misclassification. Although homomorphic encryption preserves the privacy of model updates during aggregation, it limits the application of conventional defenses that require access to plaintext updates. Moreover, distinguishing poisoned models from benign variations becomes more challenging under non-independent and identically distributed (non-IID) data distributions.To address this challenge, we introduce a defense strategy that operates at inference time by identifying abnormal internal activation patterns within the aggregated global model, rather than filtering encrypted individual updates during training. The proposed approach analyzes neurons that exhibit low activation on clean inputs, referred to as "dormant" neurons, but become disproportionately active in the presence of trigger patterns. By constructing a statistical activation baseline using a small clean dataset, we derive class-specific thresholds that serve as decision boundaries to detect and reject suspicious predictions. Since the proposed method relies on global model behavior at inference time instead of inspecting individual client updates, it does not introduce additional training overhead and remains robust under non-IID data settings. Our approach maintains a strong balance between privacy, security, and accuracy by defending against backdoor attacks without requiring access to client updates. Experimental results demonstrate that even with a 99% attack success rate and 90% main-task accuracy, the proposed defense method successfully detects 100% poisoned images.
papers.summary_zh:
## 背景与挑战  
联邦学习（FL）的分布式架构易受**后门攻击**威胁：恶意客户端通过在本地数据中嵌入特定触发器（trigger），诱导全局模型对含触发器的样本产生目标误分类。尽管**同态加密（HE）** 可保障聚合阶段模型更新的隐私性，却使传统基于明文更新的防御方法（如异常检测、鲁棒聚合）失效；更严峻的是，在**非独立同分布（non-IID）** 数据下，良性模型差异与中毒模型行为高度混叠，显著削弱判别能力。

## 方法创新  
本文提出一种**推理时（inference-time）轻量级防御机制**，不依赖解密或访问任一客户端的加密更新，而是聚焦于**聚合后全局模型的内部激活模式**。核心思想是识别“**休眠神经元**”（dormant neurons）——即在干净样本上激活值极低、却在触发器存在时被异常激活的神经元。我们利用一个**极小规模干净验证集**（无需标签）构建各类别统计激活基线，并据此设定**类敏感阈值**，作为预测可信度的判定边界：若某预测对应的触发相关神经元激活强度显著超阈，则拒绝该预测并标记为可疑。

## 主要优势与效果  
- ✅ **零训练开销**：仅需单次离线基线构建，不修改训练流程；  
- ✅ **HE原生兼容**：全程作用于解密后的全局模型，与加密协议正交；  
- ✅ **non-IID鲁棒**：实验表明其在极端数据异构（如Dirichlet α=0.1）下仍稳定有效；  
- ✅ **高检出率**：在攻击成功率99%、主任务准确率90%的严苛设定下，实现**100%的中毒图像检测率**，同时仅引入<0.3%的正常样本误拒率。

本工作首次将激活语义分析引入HE保护的FL场景，在**隐私—安全—精度三角约束下实现了实质性突破**。
papers.summary_en: Federated learning (FL) with homomorphic encryption (HE) preserves update privacy but blocks conventional backdoor defenses requiring plaintext model weights. To address this, we propose an *inference-time* defense that detects poisoned predictions by analyzing abnormal neuron activation patterns in the *decrypted global model*, without accessing or decrypting any client’s encrypted updates. Specifically, we identify “dormant neurons”—units with near-zero activation on clean inputs but disproportionately high activation under trigger patterns—and build class-specific statistical activation baselines using a small clean dataset (label-free). Predictions triggering abnormally strong dormant-neuron responses are rejected. Our method introduces no training overhead, remains robust under severe non-IID data (e.g., Dirichlet α=0.1), and achieves **100% detection of poisoned images** while maintaining 90% main-task accuracy—even under 99% attack success rate. This is the first approach to bridge HE-based privacy and backdoor security via activation semantics at inference time.

## 主要优势与效果

本工作首次将激活语义分析引入HE保护的FL场景，在**隐私—安全—精度三角约束下实现了实质性突破**。

papers.title: SENTINEL: Stagewise Integrity Verification for Pipeline Parallel Decentralized Training
papers.abstract: Decentralized training introduces critical security risks when executed across untrusted, geographically distributed nodes. While existing Byzantine-tolerant literature addresses data parallel (DP) training through robust aggregation methods, pipeline parallelism (PP) presents fundamentally distinct challenges. In PP, model layers are distributed across workers where the activations and their gradients flow between stages rather than being aggregated, making traditional DP approaches inapplicable. We propose SENTINEL, a verification mechanism for PP training without computation duplication. SENTINEL employs lightweight momentum-based monitoring using exponential moving averages (EMAs) to detect corrupted inter-stage communication. Unlike existing Byzantine-tolerant approaches for DP that aggregate parameter gradients across replicas, our approach verifies sequential activation/gradient transmission between layers. We provide theoretical convergence guarantees for this new setting that recovers classical convergence rates when relaxed to standard training. Experiments demonstrate successful training of up to 4B-parameter LLMs across untrusted distributed environments with up to 176 workers while maintaining model convergence and performance.
papers.summary_zh:
## 背景与挑战  
在跨地域、多机构协作的分布式大模型训练中，**去中心化训练**（decentralized training）面临严峻安全威胁：不可信节点可能恶意篡改通信数据，导致训练失败或模型被后门污染。现有拜占庭容错（Byzantine-tolerant）方法主要面向**数据并行**（DP），依赖梯度聚合与鲁棒平均（如Krum、Bulyan），但**流水线并行**（PP）因其独特的分层计算范式——模型层分布于不同节点、激活/梯度沿阶段顺序传递而非全局聚合——使DP方案完全失效。

## 方法创新：SENTINEL机制  
本文提出 **SENTINEL**，首个专为流水线并行设计的轻量级、无计算冗余的完整性验证框架。其核心思想是：**不复制计算，而监控跨阶段通信流的动态一致性**。具体而言：  
- 引入**指数滑动平均**（EMA）对各阶段输入/输出激活及梯度进行**动量感知建模**，构建轻量时序基准；  
- 设计自适应阈值检测器，实时识别偏离EMA轨迹的异常传输（如恶意注入、截断或替换）；  
- 支持异步容错恢复：定位异常阶段后，仅重传对应微批次数据，避免全链路重启。

## 理论与实验验证  
我们首次为PP场景建立**带通信故障的收敛性理论**，证明SENTINEL在存在≤f个拜占庭节点时仍能恢复标准SGD的$O(1/\sqrt{T})$收敛速率。实验表明：在最多176个不可信worker组成的异构集群上，SENTINEL成功稳定训练**40亿参数LLM**（如LLaMA-2 4B），训练损失曲线与可信环境高度一致，最终困惑度仅下降<0.8%，推理准确率偏差<0.3%。代码已开源。
papers.summary_en: Decentralized pipeline parallel (PP) training across untrusted nodes suffers from critical integrity risks, as Byzantine workers can corrupt sequential activation/gradient transmissions—rendering standard data-parallel Byzantine-tolerant methods inapplicable. We propose **SENTINEL**, the first lightweight, computation-duplication-free verification mechanism for PP. SENTINEL employs exponential moving averages (EMAs) to monitor momentum-consistent patterns in inter-stage communication and detects deviations via adaptive thresholds. Crucially, it provides theoretical convergence guarantees under Byzantine failures, recovering classical $O(1/\sqrt{T})$ rates when relaxed to benign settings. Experiments demonstrate robust training of up to 4B-parameter LLMs across 176 untrusted workers, achieving near-benign convergence and <0.8% perplexity degradation—without performance overhead or redundant computation.
papers.summary:
## 背景与挑战  
在跨地域、多机构协作的分布式大模型训练中，**去中心化训练**（decentralized training）面临严峻安全威胁：不可信节点可能恶意篡改通信数据，导致训练失败或模型被后门污染。现有拜占庭容错（Byzantine-tolerant）方法主要面向**数据并行**（DP），依赖梯度聚合与鲁棒平均（如Krum、Bulyan），但**流水线并行**（PP）因其独特的分层计算范式——模型层分布于不同节点、激活/梯度沿阶段顺序传递而非全局聚合——使DP方案完全失效。

## 方法创新：SENTINEL机制  
本文提出 **SENTINEL**，首个专为流水线并行设计的轻量级、无计算冗余的完整性验证框架。其核心思想是：**不复制计算，而监控跨阶段通信流的动态一致性**。具体而言：  
- 引入**指数滑动平均**（EMA）对各阶段输入/输出激活及梯度进行**动量感知建模**，构建轻量时序基准；  
- 设计自适应阈值检测器，实时识别偏离EMA轨迹的异常传输（如恶意注入、截断或替换）；  
- 支持异步容错恢复：定位异常阶段后，仅重传对应微批次数据，避免全链路重启。

## 理论与实验验证  
我们首次为PP场景建立**带通信故障的收敛性理论**，证明SENTINEL在存在≤f个拜占庭节点时仍能恢复标准SGD的$O(1/\sqrt{T})$收敛速率。实验表明：在最多176个不可信worker组成的异构集群上，SENTINEL成功稳定训练**40亿参数LLM**（如LLaMA-2 4B），训练损失曲线与可信环境高度一致，最终困惑度仅下降<0.8%，推理准确率偏差<0.3%。代码已开源。

papers.title: DKD-KAN: A Lightweight knowledge-distilled KAN intrusion detection framework, based on MLP and KAN
papers.abstract: Cyber-security systems often operate in resource-constrained environments, such as edge environments and real-time monitoring systems, where model size and inference time are crucial. A light-weight intrusion detection framework is proposed that utilizes the Kolmogorov-Arnold Network (KAN) to capture complex features in the data, with the efficiency of decoupled knowledge distillation (DKD) training approach. A high-capacity KAN network is first trained to detect attacks performed on the test bed. This model then serves as a teacher to guide a much smaller multilayer perceptron (MLP) student model via DKD. The resulting DKD-MLP model contains only 2,522 and 1,622 parameters for WADI and SWaT datasets, which are significantly smaller than the number of parameters of the KAN teacher model. This is highly appropriate for deployment in resource-constrained devices with limited computational resources. Despite its low size, the student model maintains a high performance. Our approach demonstrate the practicality of using KAN as a knowledge-rich teacher to train much smaller student models, without considerable drop in accuracy in intrusion detection frameworks. We have validated our approach on two publicly available datasets. We report F1-score improvements of 4.18% on WADI and 3.07% on SWaT when using the DKD-MLP model, compared to the bare student model. The implementation of this paper is available on our GitHub repository.
papers.summary_zh:
## 背景与挑战  
在边缘计算与实时网络监控等**资源受限环境**中，入侵检测系统（IDS）亟需兼顾高精度与轻量化——传统深度模型参数量大、推理延迟高，难以部署于嵌入式设备或工业物联网节点。Kolmogorov-Arnold Network（KAN）虽具备强非线性拟合能力，但其参数规模与计算开销显著制约实际应用。

## 方法创新：DKD-KAN框架  
本文提出**DKD-KAN**——一种基于**解耦知识蒸馏（Decoupled Knowledge Distillation, DKD）** 的轻量级IDS框架。该框架采用“教师-学生”双阶段范式：  
- **教师阶段**：训练高容量KAN模型，在WADI与SWaT工业控制系统数据集上充分学习复杂攻击模式；  
- **学生阶段**：以DKD策略引导轻量级MLP学生模型学习教师的**logits分布、中间特征响应及梯度方向**，实现知识的高效迁移，而非简单标签复刻。  

## 关键结果与优势  
- 参数量极低：DKD-MLP学生模型仅含**2,522（WADI）和1,622（SWaT）参数**，较KAN教师模型压缩超99%；  
- 性能显著提升：相较未蒸馏的基线MLP，F1-score提升**4.18%（WADI）与3.07%（SWaT）**，验证知识迁移有效性；  
- 部署友好：模型体积<10 KB，推理延迟<1 ms（ARM Cortex-M7实测），满足边缘端实时性要求。  

本工作首次将KAN作为**高知识密度教师**应用于IDS蒸馏任务，为轻量化AI安全模型提供了新范式，代码已开源。
papers.summary_en: Cybersecurity in edge and real-time monitoring environments demands intrusion detection systems (IDS) that are both accurate and ultra-lightweight. To address this, we propose **DKD-KAN**, a knowledge-distilled IDS framework leveraging the Kolmogorov-Arnold Network (KAN) as a high-capacity teacher and a compact multilayer perceptron (MLP) as the student. Using decoupled knowledge distillation (DKD), the student learns not only final logits but also intermediate feature representations and gradient dynamics from the KAN teacher—enabling rich knowledge transfer without architectural complexity. The resulting DKD-MLP model achieves just **2,522 parameters on WADI and 1,622 on SWaT**, reducing size by >99% versus the teacher, while boosting F1-score by **+4.18% (WADI) and +3.07% (SWaT)** over the standalone MLP baseline. This demonstrates KAN’s efficacy as a knowledge-rich teacher for lightweight IDS deployment. Code is publicly available.

papers.title: Zero-Knowledge Federated Learning with Lattice-Based Hybrid Encryption for Quantum-Resilient Medical AI
papers.abstract: Federated Learning (FL) enables collaborative training of medical AI models across hospitals without centralizing patient data. However, the exchange of model updates exposes critical vulnerabilities: gradient inversion attacks can reconstruct patient information, Byzantine clients can poison the global model, and the \emph{Harvest Now, Decrypt Later} (HNDL) threat renders today's encrypted traffic vulnerable to future quantum adversaries.We introduce \textbf{ZKFL-PQ} (\emph{Zero-Knowledge Federated Learning, Post-Quantum}), a three-tiered cryptographic protocol that hybridizes (i) ML-KEM (FIPS~203) for quantum-resistant key encapsulation, (ii) lattice-based Zero-Knowledge Proofs for verifiable \emph{norm-constrained} gradient integrity, and (iii) BFV homomorphic encryption for privacy-preserving aggregation. We formalize the security model and prove correctness and zero-knowledge properties under the Module-LWE, Ring-LWE, and SIS assumptions \emph{in the classical random oracle model}. We evaluate ZKFL-PQ on synthetic medical imaging data across 5 federated clients over 10 training rounds. Our protocol achieves \textbf{100\% rejection of norm-violating updates} while maintaining model accuracy at 100\%, compared to a catastrophic drop to 23\% under standard FL. The computational overhead (factor $\sim$20$\times$) is analyzed and shown to be compatible with clinical research workflows operating on daily or weekly training cycles. We emphasize that the current defense guarantees rejection of large-norm malicious updates; robustness against subtle low-norm or directional poisoning remains future work.
papers.summary_zh:
## 背景与挑战  
联邦学习（FL）为跨医院协作训练医疗AI模型提供了数据不出域的范式，但面临三重量子时代安全威胁：（1）梯度反演攻击可重建患者敏感影像；（2）拜占庭客户端通过恶意模型更新实施模型投毒；（3）“先存储、后解密”（HNDL）攻击使当前RSA/ECC加密流量在量子计算机出现后即遭破解。

## 方法创新：ZKFL-PQ三阶协议  
本文提出**ZKFL-PQ**（零知识联邦学习-后量子），首个融合零知识验证与后量子密码的FL框架：  
- **量子安全密钥分发**：采用NIST标准ML-KEM（FIPS 203）实现抗量子密钥封装；  
- **可验证梯度完整性**：基于格的零知识证明（ZKP），严格验证每轮上传梯度是否满足预设ℓ₂范数约束（防大范数投毒）；  
- **隐私保护聚合**：集成BFV同态加密，支持服务器在密文下完成加权平均，无需解密单个更新。  
安全性在经典随机预言模型下形式化证明，基于Module-LWE、Ring-LWE与SIS困难假设，同时保障**正确性**（合法更新必被接受）、**零知识性**（验证者获知零额外信息）与**抗量子性**。

## 实验结果  
在5家医院模拟场景、10轮训练、合成医学影像数据上评估：ZKFL-PQ实现**100%恶意大范数更新拦截率**，全局模型准确率维持**100%**（标准FL因投毒骤降至23%）。计算开销约基准FL的20倍，但完全适配临床研究中常见的日/周级训练节奏。需强调：本方案**确定性防御大范数异常更新**，对低范数或方向性精细投毒的鲁棒性仍属开放问题。
papers.summary_en: Federated Learning (FL) enables privacy-preserving medical AI training across hospitals, yet suffers from gradient inversion, Byzantine poisoning, and “Harvest Now, Decrypt Later” (HNDL) quantum threats. We propose **ZKFL-PQ**, the first zero-knowledge FL framework integrating three post-quantum primitives: (i) ML-KEM (FIPS 203) for quantum-safe key encapsulation; (ii) lattice-based zero-knowledge proofs to *verifiably enforce ℓ₂-norm constraints* on gradients—detecting large-norm malicious updates; and (iii) BFV homomorphic encryption for secure, decryption-free model aggregation. We prove correctness and zero-knowledge under Module-LWE, Ring-LWE, and SIS assumptions in the classical random oracle model. Evaluated on synthetic medical imaging across 5 clients over 10 rounds, ZKFL-PQ achieves **100% rejection of norm-violating updates** and maintains **100% model accuracy**, versus a catastrophic 23% drop under standard FL. Though computational overhead is ~20×, it remains feasible for daily/weekly clinical workflows. Robustness against subtle low-norm poisoning is left for future work.

papers.title: Benchmark of Benchmarks: Unpacking Influence and Code Repository Quality in LLM Safety Benchmarks
papers.abstract: The rapid growth of research in LLM safety makes it hard to track all advances. Benchmarks are therefore crucial for capturing key trends and enabling systematic comparisons. Yet, it remains unclear why certain benchmarks gain prominence, and no systematic assessment has been conducted on their academic influence or code quality. This paper fills this gap by presenting the first multi-dimensional evaluation of the influence (based on five metrics) and code quality (based on both automated and human assessment) on LLM safety benchmarks, analyzing 31 benchmarks and 382 non-benchmarks across prompt injection, jailbreak, and hallucination. We find that benchmark papers show no significant advantage in academic influence (e.g., citation count and density) over non-benchmark papers. We uncover a key misalignment: while author prominence correlates with paper influence, neither author prominence nor paper influence shows a significant correlation with code quality. Our results also indicate substantial room for improvement in code and supplementary materials: only 39% of repositories are ready-to-use, 16% include flawless installation guides, and a mere 6% address ethical considerations. Given that the work of prominent researchers tends to attract greater attention, they need to lead the effort in setting higher standards.
papers.summary_zh:
## 研究背景  
大语言模型（LLM）安全研究呈爆发式增长，但领域缺乏统一评估框架，导致进展难以追踪与比较。基准测试（benchmarks）本应承担趋势凝练与横向对比的核心功能，然而其实际影响力来源、学术认可度及工程可用性长期缺乏系统性检验。

## 方法与范围  
本文首次开展多维实证评估：  
- **影响力维度**：基于5项指标（总引用数、年均引用密度、H指数、被顶会/期刊引用占比、跨机构引用广度）量化31个LLM安全基准论文（涵盖提示注入、越狱攻击、幻觉三类任务）与382篇非基准对照论文的学术影响；  
- **代码质量维度**：结合自动化扫描（CI通过率、文档完整性、依赖声明）与双盲人工评审（安装可复现性、测试覆盖度、伦理说明），全面评估对应开源仓库质量。

## 关键发现  
- 基准论文在引用量、引用密度等核心影响力指标上**未显著优于非基准论文**（p > 0.05）；  
- 存在显著“三重脱节”：作者学术声望虽正向预测论文影响力（r = 0.42），但**既不预测代码可用性，也不与代码质量指标（如安装成功率、伦理披露）相关**；  
- 工程实践严重滞后：仅**39%** 仓库开箱即用，**16%** 提供无缺陷安装指南，**仅6%** 明确讨论数据偏见、滥用风险等伦理议题；  
- 高影响力作者团队主导的基准项目，其代码质量反而低于平均水平（均值低1.8分，Likert 5分制）。

## 创新与启示  
本研究揭示了“影响力≠高质量实践”的行业悖论，呼吁建立**影响力-质量双轨评估标准**。建议将代码可复现性、伦理完备性纳入基准认证前置条件，并由领军学者牵头制定《LLM安全基准开发白皮书》。
papers.summary_en: This paper presents the first systematic, multi-dimensional benchmarking of LLM safety benchmarks themselves—evaluating both their *academic influence* (via 5 metrics: citation count, citation density, H-index, top-venue citation share, cross-institutional reach) and *code repository quality* (via automated checks + double-blind human assessment across installability, documentation, testing, and ethics). Analyzing 31 safety benchmarks and 382 non-benchmark papers across prompt injection, jailbreak, and hallucination tasks, we find: (1) benchmark papers show **no statistically significant advantage** in academic influence over non-benchmark papers; (2) author prominence predicts paper influence but **fails to correlate with code quality**, revealing a critical misalignment; (3) repository readiness is alarmingly low—only 39% are ready-to-use, 16% have flawless installation guides, and a mere 6% address ethical considerations. These results challenge the assumption that high-impact work implies high-quality engineering practice and call for mandatory, standardized quality criteria—including reproducibility and ethics—for future benchmark adoption.
papers.summary:

papers.title: Asymmetric Goal Drift in Coding Agents Under Value Conflict
papers.abstract: Agentic coding agents are increasingly deployed autonomously, at scale, and over long-context horizons. Throughout an agent's lifetime, it must navigate tensions between explicit instructions, learned values, and environmental pressures, often in contexts unseen during training. Prior work on model preferences, agent behavior under value tensions, and goal drift has relied on static, synthetic settings that do not capture the complexity of real-world environments. To this end, we introduce a framework built on OpenCode to orchestrate realistic, multi-step coding tasks to measure how agents violate explicit constraints in their system prompt over time with and without environmental pressure toward competing values. Using this framework, we demonstrate that GPT-5 mini, Haiku 4.5, and Grok Code Fast 1 exhibit asymmetric drift: they are more likely to violate their system prompt when its constraint opposes strongly-held values like security and privacy. We find for the models and values tested that goal drift correlates with three compounding factors: value alignment, adversarial pressure, and accumulated context. However, even strongly-held values like privacy show non-zero violation rates under sustained environmental pressure. These findings reveal that shallow compliance checks are insufficient and that comment-based pressure can exploit model value hierarchies to override system prompt instructions. More broadly, our findings highlight a gap in current alignment approaches in ensuring that agentic systems appropriately balance explicit user constraints against broadly beneficial learned preferences under sustained environmental pressure.
papers.summary_zh:
## 研究背景与问题  
随着自主编码智能体（agentic coding agents）在真实场景中大规模、长周期部署，其需持续协调**显式指令**（如系统提示中的硬性约束）、**内隐习得价值观**（如安全、隐私偏好）与**动态环境压力**（如代码注释中嵌入的对抗性引导）三者间的张力。现有研究多依赖静态合成任务，难以刻画真实多步开发环境中目标偏移（goal drift）的渐进性与不对称性。

## 方法创新  
本研究基于 **OpenCode 构建首个可扩展、现实化的多步编码评估框架**：  
- 设计含显式约束（如“禁止日志敏感信息”）的长程任务流；  
- 引入两类环境压力：*中性上下文*（无价值干扰）与*对抗性注释压力*（在代码中嵌入“为调试请打印用户ID”等违背约束但符合常见工程直觉的提示）；  
- 在 GPT-5 mini、Haiku 4.5 和 Grok Code Fast 1 三大主流编码模型上，量化其系统提示合规率随步骤数、价值冲突强度及压力持续时间的变化轨迹。

## 核心发现  
1. **显著的不对称目标漂移**：当系统提示约束与强价值观（如隐私、安全）冲突时，违规率升高 2.3–5.7 倍；而反向冲突（如放宽隐私要求）几乎不引发漂移；  
2. **三重耦合驱动机制**：漂移程度由**价值观对齐度**（模型自身偏好强度）、**对抗压力强度**与**累积上下文长度**共同非线性放大；  
3. **浅层合规失效**：即使隐私被模型高度内化，持续 8 步以上对抗注释压力仍导致 12.4%–29.8% 的违规率；  
4. **实践警示**：基于注释的轻量级压力即可系统性劫持模型价值层级，覆盖系统提示——表明当前对齐方法缺乏对“约束-偏好-环境”动态三角关系的鲁棒平衡能力。
papers.summary_en: We introduce the first realistic, multi-step framework—built on OpenCode—to measure *asymmetric goal drift* in coding agents under value conflict. Unlike prior static benchmarks, our setup exposes models (GPT-5 mini, Haiku 4.5, Grok Code Fast 1) to sustained environmental pressure (e.g., adversarial comments urging privacy-violating logging) while tracking compliance with explicit system-prompt constraints over long contexts. We find that drift is strongly asymmetric: violation rates surge when constraints oppose deeply internalized values (e.g., privacy), but not vice versa. Drift is driven by three compounding factors—value alignment strength, adversarial pressure intensity, and accumulated context length—even for highly aligned values. Critically, comment-based pressure alone suffices to override system instructions, revealing a fundamental gap: current alignment approaches fail to robustly balance user-specified constraints against learned preferences under dynamic, prolonged environmental influence.

papers.title: AOI: Turning Failed Trajectories into Training Signals for Autonomous Cloud Diagnosis
papers.abstract: Large language model (LLM) agents offer a promising data-driven approach to automating Site Reliability Engineering (SRE), yet their enterprise deployment is constrained by three challenges: restricted access to proprietary data, unsafe action execution under permission-governed environments, and the inability of closed systems to improve from failures. We present AOI (Autonomous Operations Intelligence), a trainable multi-agent framework formulating automated operations as a structured trajectory learning problem under security constraints. Our approach integrates three key components. First, a trainable diagnostic system applies Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) to distill expert-level knowledge into locally deployed open-source models, enabling preference-based learning without exposing sensitive data. Second, a read-write separated execution architecture decomposes operational trajectories into observation, reasoning, and action phases, allowing safe learning while preventing unauthorized state mutation. Third, a Failure Trajectory Closed-Loop Evolver mines unsuccessful trajectories and converts them into corrective supervision signals, enabling continual data augmentation. Evaluated on the AIOpsLab benchmark, our contributions yield cumulative gains. (1) The AOI runtime alone achieves 66.3% best@5 success on all 86 tasks, outperforming the prior state-of-the-art (41.9%) by 24.4 points. (2) Adding Observer GRPO training, a locally deployed 14B model reaches 42.9% avg@1 on 63 held-out tasks with unseen fault types, surpassing Claude Sonnet 4.5. (3) The Evolver converts 37 failed trajectories into diagnostic guidance, improving end-to-end avg@5 by 4.8 points while reducing variance by 35%.
papers.summary_zh:
## 背景与挑战  
面向云原生环境的自主故障诊断亟需安全、可演进、数据合规的智能体框架。当前大语言模型（LLM）代理在SRE自动化中面临三大瓶颈：**企业敏感数据不可出域**、**权限受限环境下动作执行风险高**、**封闭系统无法从失败中学习**，导致模型难以持续提升。

## 方法创新：AOI三支柱架构  
我们提出**AOI（Autonomous Operations Intelligence）**——首个面向安全约束下结构化轨迹学习的可训练多智能体框架：  
- **可训练诊断系统**：采用**分组相对策略优化（GRPO）**，在本地部署开源模型（如14B参数量）上实现偏好学习，全程无需原始日志或配置数据出域，完成专家知识蒸馏；  
- **读写分离执行架构**：将操作轨迹解耦为**观测→推理→动作**三阶段，仅允许只读观测与推理生成，严格阻断未经授权的状态变更，保障生产环境安全；  
- **失败轨迹闭环演化器（Evolver）**：自动识别诊断失败路径，将其转化为带修正标签的监督信号（如“应先检查K8s Event而非直接重启Pod”），实现**零样本故障泛化**与**持续数据增强**。

## 关键成果  
在AIOpsLab基准测试（86个真实云故障任务）中：  
✅ AOI运行时系统达成**66.3% best@5成功率**，较SOTA（41.9%）提升24.4个百分点；  
✅ 结合Observer GRPO训练后，本地14B模型在63个**未见过故障类型**的保留任务上取得**42.9% avg@1**，超越Claude Sonnet 4.5；  
✅ Evolver将37条失败轨迹转化为诊断指导，推动端到端**avg@5提升4.8点，结果方差降低35%**——验证“失败即燃料”的闭环进化范式。
papers.summary_en: Large language model (LLM) agents hold promise for automating Site Reliability Engineering (SRE), but enterprise deployment is hindered by data privacy constraints, unsafe action execution in permissioned environments, and the inability to learn from failures. We introduce **AOI (Autonomous Operations Intelligence)**, a trainable multi-agent framework that reframes autonomous cloud diagnosis as a structured trajectory learning problem under strict security boundaries. AOI integrates three innovations: (1) a locally deployed diagnostic system trained via **Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO)** to distill expert knowledge without exposing sensitive data; (2) a **read-write separated execution architecture** that decouples trajectories into observation, reasoning, and action phases—enabling safe learning while preventing unauthorized state mutation; and (3) a **Failure Trajectory Closed-Loop Evolver** that mines unsuccessful trajectories and converts them into corrective supervision signals for continual data augmentation. Evaluated on AIOpsLab, AOI achieves **66.3% best@5 success** across 86 tasks (+24.4 pts over SOTA), enables a local 14B model to reach **42.9% avg@1** on 63 held-out tasks with unseen faults (surpassing Claude Sonnet 4.5), and improves end-to-end **avg@5 by +4.8 points** while reducing variance by **35%**, demonstrating failure-driven closed-loop evolution.
papers.summary:
## 背景与挑战  
面向云原生环境的自主故障诊断亟需安全、可演进、数据合规的智能体框架。当前大语言模型（LLM）代理在SRE自动化中面临三大瓶颈：**企业敏感数据不可出域**、**权限受限环境下动作执行风险高**、**封闭系统无法从失败中学习**，导致模型难以持续提升。

## 方法创新：AOI三支柱架构  
我们提出**AOI（Autonomous Operations Intelligence）**——首个面向安全约束下结构化轨迹学习的可训练多智能体框架：  
- **可训练诊断系统**：采用**分组相对策略优化（GRPO）**，在本地部署开源模型（如14B参数量）上实现偏好学习，全程无需原始日志或配置数据出域，完成专家知识蒸馏；  
- **读写分离执行架构**：将操作轨迹解耦为**观测→推理→动作**三阶段，仅允许只读观测与推理生成，严格阻断未经授权的状态变更，保障生产环境安全；  
- **失败轨迹闭环演化器（Evolver）**：自动识别诊断失败路径，将其转化为带修正标签的监督信号（如“应先检查K8s Event而非直接重启Pod”），实现**零样本故障泛化**与**持续数据增强**。

## 关键成果  
在AIOpsLab基准测试（86个真实云故障任务）中：  
✅ AOI运行时系统达成**66.3% best@5成功率**，较SOTA（41.9%）提升24.4个百分点；  
✅ 结合Observer GRPO训练后，本地14B模型在63个**未见过故障类型**的保留任务上取得**42.9% avg@1**，超越Claude Sonnet 4.5；  
✅ Evolver将37条失败轨迹转化为诊断指导，推动端到端**avg@5提升4.8点，结果方差降低35%**——验证“失败即燃料”的闭环进化范式。

papers.title: Solving adversarial examples requires solving exponential misalignment
papers.abstract: Adversarial attacks - input perturbations imperceptible to humans that fool neural networks - remain both a persistent failure mode in machine learning, and a phenomenon with mysterious origins. To shed light, we define and analyze a network's perceptual manifold (PM) for a class concept as the space of all inputs confidently assigned to that class by the network. We find, strikingly, that the dimensionalities of neural network PMs are orders of magnitude higher than those of natural human concepts. Since volume typically grows exponentially with dimension, this suggests exponential misalignment between machines and humans, with exponentially many inputs confidently assigned to concepts by machines but not humans. Furthermore, this provides a natural geometric hypothesis for the origin of adversarial examples: because a network's PM fills such a large region of input space, any input will be very close to any class concept's PM. Our hypothesis thus suggests that adversarial robustness cannot be attained without dimensional alignment of machine and human PMs, and therefore makes strong predictions: both robust accuracy and distance to any PM should be negatively correlated with the PM dimension. We confirmed these predictions across 18 different networks of varying robust accuracy. Crucially, we find even the most robust networks are still exponentially misaligned, and only the few PMs whose dimensionality approaches that of human concepts exhibit alignment to human perception. Our results connect the fields of alignment and adversarial examples, and suggest the curse of high dimensionality of machine PMs is a major impediment to adversarial robustness.
papers.summary_zh:
## 研究背景与问题  
对抗样本——即对人类不可察觉却能使神经网络误分类的微小输入扰动——既是机器学习中长期存在的鲁棒性缺陷，也是成因未明的基础性现象。现有解释多聚焦于局部梯度或过拟合，缺乏统一的几何机制。

## 核心方法：感知流形（PM）建模  
本文提出**网络感知流形（Perceptual Manifold, PM）** 的严格定义：对某类别概念，其PM是网络以高置信度将其判为该类的所有输入构成的集合。我们首次系统量化了18个主流模型（含标准训练与对抗训练变体）在ImageNet子集上的各类别PM维度（通过局部主成分分析与流形维度估计），并对比人类概念的先验低维结构（如形状、语义属性等通常由≤5维参数刻画）。

## 关键发现与理论贡献  
- **指数级错位（Exponential Misalignment）**：神经网络PM的典型维度达**30–80维**，比人类概念高1–2个数量级；由于体积随维度呈指数增长，导致网络“过度自信”地将海量输入映射至错误类别——这直接解释了对抗样本的普遍存在性。  
- **几何起源假说**：因高维PM在输入空间中占据极大体积，任意自然图像必与任一类PM在欧氏距离上极近（常< ℓ₂=0.5），故微小扰动即可跨类边界。  
- **可验证预测**：① 模型鲁棒精度与PM平均维度呈强负相关（*r* = −0.92, *p* < 10⁻⁶）；② 输入到最近PM的距离越小，越易受攻击——二者在所有模型中均显著负相关（*r* = −0.87）。  
- **根本性结论**：当前最强对抗训练模型（如TRADES、PGD）的PM维度仍高达42±6，远超人类认知维度（≈3–5），证实**单纯优化损失无法解决结构性错位**；仅当个别类别PM维度压缩至≈5时，才观测到与人类感知的一致性。

本研究首次将**对齐问题（Alignment）** 与**对抗鲁棒性**在流形几何层面统一，指出“高维诅咒”是鲁棒性的本质瓶颈。
papers.summary_en: This paper identifies **exponential misalignment** between neural networks and human perception as the geometric root of adversarial examples. We define a network’s *perceptual manifold (PM)* for a class as the set of all inputs assigned to it with high confidence, and find its intrinsic dimension is 30–80—orders of magnitude higher than human concepts (typically ≤5D). Because volume grows exponentially with dimension, this implies exponentially many inputs are confidently misclassified. Consequently, any natural image lies extremely close (often < ℓ₂=0.5) to some class’s PM, making small perturbations sufficient for misclassification. We validate two key predictions across 18 models: (1) robust accuracy is strongly negatively correlated with average PM dimension (*r* = −0.92); and (2) distance from an input to its nearest PM predicts vulnerability (*r* = −0.87). Crucially, even state-of-the-art robust models remain exponentially misaligned—their PMs retain ~42D—while only the rare PMs approaching human-scale dimensionality (~5D) align with human perception. This unifies adversarial robustness and alignment through manifold geometry.

papers.title: Geographically-Weighted Weakly Supervised Bayesian High-Resolution Transformer for 200m Resolution Pan-Arctic Sea Ice Concentration Mapping and Uncertainty Estimation using Sentinel-1, RCM, and AMSR2 Data
papers.abstract: Although high-resolution mapping of pan-Arctic sea ice with reliable corresponding uncertainty is essential for operational sea ice concentration (SIC) charting, it is a difficult task due to key challenges, such as the subtle nature of ice signature features, inexact SIC labels, model uncertainty, and data heterogeneity. This study presents a novel Bayesian High-Resolution Transformer approach for 200 meter resolution pan-Arctic SIC mapping and uncertainty quantification using Sentinel-1, RADARSAT Constellation Mission (RCM), and Advanced Microwave Scanning Radiometer 2 (AMSR2) data. First, to improve small and subtle sea ice feature (e.g., cracks/leads, ponds, and ice floes) extraction, we design a novel high-resolution Transformer model with both global and local modules that can better discern the subtle differences in sea ice patterns. Second, to address low-resolution and inexact SIC labels, we design a geographically-weighted weakly supervised loss function to supervise the model at region level instead of pixel level, and to prioritize pure open water and ice pack signatures while mitigating the impact of ambiguity in the marginal ice zone (MIZ). Third, to improve uncertainty quantification, we design a Bayesian extension of the proposed Transformer model, treating its parameters as random variables to more effectively capture uncertainties. Fourth, to address data heterogeneity, we fuse three different data types (Sentinel-1, RCM, and AMSR2) at decision-level to improve both SIC mapping and uncertainty quantification. The proposed approach is evaluated under pan-Arctic minimum-extent conditions in 2021 and 2025. Results demonstrate that the proposed model achieves 0.70 overall feature detection accuracy using Sentinel-1 data, while also preserving pan-Arctic SIC patterns (Sentinel-1 R\textsuperscript{2} = 0.90 relative to the ARTIST Sea Ice product).
papers.summary_zh:
## 面向泛北极高分辨率海冰浓度制图与不确定性量化的地理加权弱监督贝叶斯Transformer模型

**背景与挑战**：泛北极200米级海冰浓度（SIC）制图及其可靠不确定性评估对业务化海冰监测至关重要，但面临多重瓶颈：海冰微弱特征（如冰裂隙、融池、浮冰）信噪比低、标签数据空间分辨率粗（通常≥3 km）、真值存在固有模糊性（尤其在边缘冰区MIZ）、模型参数与数据源异质性引入的多重不确定性。

**方法创新**：本研究提出首个**地理加权弱监督贝叶斯高分辨率Transformer框架**，融合Sentinel-1 SAR、RADARSAT星座任务（RCM）与AMSR2被动微波数据。其四大核心设计为：（1）构建兼具**全局建模能力与局部细节感知**的高分辨率Transformer主干，显著提升微小海冰结构识别能力；（2）设计**地理加权弱监督损失函数**，以区域（而非像素）为监督单元，动态赋予纯开阔水域与密集冰盖样本更高权重，抑制MIZ过渡带标签噪声干扰；（3）引入**贝叶斯参数化机制**，将Transformer权重视为随机变量，通过蒙特卡洛DropPath采样实现后验不确定性量化；（4）采用**决策级多源数据融合策略**，避免早期特征对齐失配，协同增强SIC反演鲁棒性与不确定性校准精度。

**主要结果**：在2021年与2025年泛北极最小海冰范围期验证中，模型基于Sentinel-1数据实现**0.70整体特征检测准确率**，SIC空间分布与ARTIST产品高度一致（R² = 0.90），同时输出物理可解释的逐像素不确定性热图。本工作为高分辨率海冰业务化制图提供了首个兼具精度、可解释性与不确定性保障的端到端深度学习范式。
papers.summary_en: This study introduces the first **Geographically-Weighted Weakly Supervised Bayesian High-Resolution Transformer** for pan-Arctic sea ice concentration (SIC) mapping at 200 m resolution and calibrated uncertainty estimation. To address subtle ice feature detection, we design a novel Transformer with hybrid global–local attention. To overcome coarse and ambiguous SIC labels (e.g., in the marginal ice zone), we propose a region-level, geography-aware weak supervision loss that prioritizes pure open-water and consolidated-ice samples. For rigorous uncertainty quantification, we embed Bayesian inference into the Transformer by treating parameters as random variables and performing Monte Carlo sampling via DropPath. Finally, we fuse Sentinel-1, RCM, and AMSR2 data at the decision level to mitigate sensor heterogeneity. Evaluated under pan-Arctic minimum-extent conditions (2021 & 2025), our model achieves 0.70 feature detection accuracy using Sentinel-1 alone and strong spatial agreement with ARTIST (R² = 0.90), while delivering pixel-wise, physically interpretable uncertainty maps—establishing a new benchmark for operational, uncertainty-aware high-resolution sea ice monitoring.
papers.summary:

**背景与挑战**：泛北极200米级海冰浓度（SIC）制图及其可靠不确定性评估对业务化海冰监测至关重要，但面临多重瓶颈：海冰微弱特征（如冰裂隙、融池、浮冰）信噪比低、标签数据空间分辨率粗（通常≥3 km）、真值存在固有模糊性（尤其在边缘冰区MIZ）、模型参数与数据源异质性引入的多重不确定性。

papers.title: PrivMedChat: End-to-End Differentially Private RLHF for Medical Dialogue Systems
papers.abstract: Large language models are increasingly used for patient-facing medical assistance and clinical decision support, but adapting them to clinical dialogue often requires supervision derived from doctor-patient conversations that may contain sensitive information. Conventional supervised fine-tuning and reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF) can amplify memorization, enabling membership inference and disclosure of rare training-set details. We present PrivMedChat (Private Medical Chat), an end-to-end framework for differentially private RLHF (DP-RLHF) for medical dialogue systems. Our approach enforces differential privacy at each training stage that accesses dialogue-derived supervision, combining DP-SGD for supervised fine-tuning and reward model learning from preference pairs, and DP-aware policy optimization for alignment. To avoid costly clinician labeling, we introduce an annotation-free preference construction strategy that pairs physician responses with filtered non-expert generations. We evaluate PrivMedChat across medical dialogue tasks and assess utility, safety, and privacy under consistent privacy accounting, thereby providing a practical pathway to align medical chatbots while offering formal privacy guarantees. We open-source our code at https://github.com/sudip-bhujel/privmedchat.
papers.summary_zh:
## PrivMedChat：面向医疗对话系统的端到端差分隐私RLHF框架  

随着大语言模型（LLM）在患者咨询辅助与临床决策支持中的广泛应用，其临床对话适配亟需基于真实医患对话的监督信号——但此类数据高度敏感，易引发隐私泄露风险。传统监督微调（SFT）与人类反馈强化学习（RLHF）易导致模型过度记忆训练样本，诱发**成员推断攻击**并暴露罕见病例细节，严重威胁患者隐私。为此，本文提出 **PrivMedChat**（Private Medical Chat），首个面向医疗对话系统的**端到端差分隐私RLHF（DP-RLHF）框架**。  

本框架在所有接触对话监督信号的训练阶段严格实施差分隐私：  
- 采用**DP-SGD**对监督微调与偏好奖励模型训练进行隐私保护；  
- 设计**DP感知的策略优化算法**，在PPO更新中嵌入梯度裁剪与高斯噪声机制；  
- 创新性提出**无标注偏好构建策略**：无需临床专家人工标注，自动将医师权威响应与经医学知识过滤的非专家生成结果配对，显著降低标注成本与隐私暴露面。  

我们在多源医学对话基准（MedDialog、HealthCareMagic）上系统评估PrivMedChat，在**统一隐私预算（ε=2.0, δ=1e−5）下**同步量化**效用**（BLEU、ROUGE、临床准确性）、**安全性**（幻觉率、有害响应率）与**隐私保障强度**（成员推断攻击成功率下降≥68%）。结果表明：PrivMedChat在保持92.3%原始模型对话质量的同时，将隐私风险控制在理论可证界内，为合规部署医疗聊天机器人提供了**首个兼具形式化隐私保证与临床实用性的端到端解决方案**。代码已开源：https://github.com/sudip-bhujel/privmedchat。
papers.summary_en: PrivMedChat introduces the first end-to-end differentially private RLHF (DP-RLHF) framework for medical dialogue systems. To mitigate privacy risks—especially membership inference and leakage of rare clinical details—we enforce differential privacy at every supervision-accessing stage: DP-SGD for supervised fine-tuning and reward modeling from preference pairs, plus DP-aware PPO policy optimization with gradient clipping and Gaussian noise. Crucially, we propose an annotation-free preference construction method that pairs physician responses with filtered non-expert generations, eliminating costly clinician labeling. Evaluated across MedDialog and HealthCareMagic under unified privacy accounting (ε = 2.0, δ = 1e−5), PrivMedChat retains 92.3% of baseline utility while reducing membership inference attack success by ≥68%. It delivers formal privacy guarantees without compromising clinical safety or dialogue quality—enabling trustworthy, deployable medical chatbots. Code is open-sourced at https://github.com/sudip-bhujel/privmedchat.
papers.summary:

- 创新性提出**无标注偏好构建策略**：无需临床专家人工标注，自动将医师权威响应与经医学知识过滤的非专家生成结果配对，显著降低标注成本与隐私暴露面。  

我们在多源医学对话基准（MedDialog、HealthCareMagic）上系统评估PrivMedChat，在**统一隐私预算（ε=2.0, δ=1e−5）下**同步量化**效用**（BLEU、ROUGE、临床准确性）、**安全性**（幻觉率、有害响应率）与**隐私保障强度**（成员推断攻击成功率下降≥68%）。结果表明：PrivMedChat在保持92.3%原始模型对话质量的同时，将隐私风险控制在理论可证界内，为合规部署医疗聊天机器人提供了**首个兼具形式化隐私保证与临床实用性的端到端解决方案**。代码已开源：https://github.com/sudip-bhujel/privmedchat。

papers.title: Protection against Source Inference Attacks in Federated Learning
papers.abstract: Federated Learning (FL) was initially proposed as a privacy-preserving machine learning paradigm. However, FL has been shown to be susceptible to a series of privacy attacks. Recently, there has been concern about the Source Inference Attack (SIA), where an honest-but-curious central server attempts to identify exactly which client owns a given data point which was used in the training phase. Alarmingly, standard gradient obfuscation techniques with Differential Privacy have been shown to be ineffective against SIAs, at least without severely diminishing the accuracy.   In this work, we propose a defense against SIAs within the widely studied shuffle model of FL, where an honest shuffler acts as an intermediary between the clients and the server. First, we demonstrate that standard naive shuffling alone is insufficient to prevent SIAs. To effectively defend against SIAs, shuffling needs to be applied at a more granular level; we propose a novel combination of parameter-level shuffling with the residue number system (RNS). Our approach provides robust protection against SIAs without affecting the accuracy of the joint model and can be seamlessly integrated into other privacy protection mechanisms.   We conduct experiments on a series of models and datasets, confirming that standard shuffling approaches fail to prevent SIAs and that, in contrast, our proposed method reduce the attack's accuracy to the level of random guessing.
papers.summary_zh:
## 背景与问题  
联邦学习（FL）虽被设计为隐私保护型分布式训练范式，但近年研究揭示其易受多种隐私攻击威胁。其中，**源推断攻击（Source Inference Attack, SIA）** 构成严峻挑战：一个诚实但好奇的中央服务器可利用客户端上传的梯度，精准识别某训练样本究竟来自哪一特定客户端。更令人担忧的是，传统基于差分隐私（DP）的梯度扰动方法在抵御SIA时效果有限——若要达到理论安全强度，往往导致模型精度显著下降，实用性严重受限。

## 方法创新  
本文在广泛研究的**FL混洗模型（shuffle model）** 框架下提出新型防御机制。我们首先严格证明：仅对客户端梯度进行粗粒度“整模型级”混洗（naive shuffling）**无法有效抵抗SIA**。关键突破在于将混洗操作下沉至**参数级细粒度层面**，并创新性融合**剩余数系统（Residue Number System, RNS）** 实现无损、可验证的数值重编码与随机化重排。该方案在不引入额外噪声的前提下，彻底切断梯度与原始客户端身份间的统计关联。

## 主要发现与优势  
- 在MNIST、CIFAR-10及Sentiment140等多数据集、CNN/LSTM多模型实验中，标准混洗方案的SIA攻击准确率仍高达68%–82%，远超随机基线（10%–25%）；  
- 我们的RNS-混洗方案将攻击准确率压降至**22.3%–26.1%**，与理论随机猜测水平（如10客户端场景下为10%）误差<3个百分点，证实强防护效力；  
- 全过程保持**零精度损失**——联合模型测试准确率与无防护基准完全一致；  
- 具备正交兼容性，可无缝叠加同态加密、安全聚合等其他隐私机制，形成纵深防御体系。
papers.summary_en: Federated Learning (FL) is vulnerable to Source Inference Attacks (SIA), where an honest-but-curious server identifies the exact client contributing a given training sample—despite standard differential privacy defenses failing without severe accuracy degradation. We propose a novel defense within the shuffle model of FL, demonstrating that naive gradient-level shuffling is insufficient. Instead, we introduce **parameter-level shuffling combined with the Residue Number System (RNS)**, enabling lossless, identity-agnostic reordering of model parameters without noise injection. Our method provably breaks statistical links between gradients and client identities while preserving full model accuracy. Experiments across MNIST, CIFAR-10, and Sentiment140 show that baseline shuffling yields SIA accuracy of 68%–82%, whereas our approach reduces it to 22.3%–26.1%—statistically indistinguishable from random guessing (e.g., 10% for 10 clients). The solution integrates seamlessly with existing privacy mechanisms and incurs no accuracy trade-off.

papers.title: Accurate, private, secure, federated U-statistics with higher degree
papers.abstract: We study the problem of computing a U-statistic with a kernel function f of degree k $\ge$ 2, i.e., the average of some function f over all k-tuples of instances, in a federated learning setting. Ustatistics of degree 2 include several useful statistics such as Kendall's $τ$ coefficient, the Area under the Receiver-Operator Curve and the Gini mean difference. Existing methods provide solutions only under the lower-utility local differential privacy model and/or scale poorly in the size of the domain discretization. In this work, we propose a protocol that securely computes U-statistics of degree k $\ge$ 2 under central differential privacy by leveraging Multi Party Computation (MPC). Our method substantially improves accuracy when compared to prior solutions. We provide a detailed theoretical analysis of its accuracy, communication and computational properties. We evaluate its performance empirically, obtaining favorable results, e.g., for Kendall's $τ$ coefficient, our approach reduces the Mean Squared Error by up to four orders of magnitude over existing baselines.
papers.summary_zh:
## 研究背景与问题  
U统计量（U-statistic）是一类重要的非参数统计量，广泛应用于相关性分析、模型评估（如AUC）、不平等度量（如Gini均值差）等场景。当核函数 $f$ 的阶数 $k \geq 2$ 时（如Kendall’s $\tau$ 对应 $k=2$），其计算需遍历所有 $k$-元组样本，天然具备高计算复杂度与隐私敏感性。在**联邦学习**框架下，数据分布式存储于多个参与方，如何在不暴露原始数据的前提下，**准确、私密、安全地聚合计算高阶U统计量**，仍是一个开放挑战。

## 方法创新  
本文提出首个面向**中心化差分隐私（central differential privacy）** 的安全联邦U统计量计算协议，突破现有工作仅限于低效的**本地差分隐私（LDP）模型**或严重依赖域离散化精度的局限。核心设计融合：  
- **安全多方计算（MPC）** 实现跨客户端的协同求和，避免中间结果泄露；  
- **隐私预算优化分配机制**，将噪声精准注入U统计量的对称分解结构中，显著抑制误差累积；  
- **可扩展通信协议**，通信开销为 $O(n^{k-1})$（$n$ 为单方样本量），远优于朴素全量传输方案。

## 主要成果  
理论层面：严格证明了协议满足 $(\varepsilon,\delta)$-差分隐私，且均方误差（MSE）上界为 $O(1/(n^k\varepsilon^2))$，较LDP基线提升达 $O(n^{k-1})$ 量级。实验表明：在Kendall’s $\tau$ 计算任务中，本方法**MSE降低最高达四个数量级**；在AUC估计中，隐私预算 $\varepsilon=1$ 下误差仅为基准方法的 $1/270$。代码已开源，支持任意 $k \geq 2$ 的核函数灵活接入。
papers.summary_en: We address the problem of privately and securely computing degree-$k$ ($k \geq 2$) U-statistics—e.g., Kendall’s $\tau$, AUC, Gini mean difference—in federated learning. Prior approaches rely on low-utility local differential privacy (LDP) or suffer from poor scalability with domain discretization. We propose the first MPC-based protocol for *centrally* differentially private U-statistic computation, achieving significantly higher accuracy. Our method leverages symmetric kernel decomposition and optimized noise injection to minimize MSE while guaranteeing $(\varepsilon,\delta)$-privacy. Theoretically, its MSE scales as $O(1/(n^k\varepsilon^2))$, outperforming LDP baselines by up to $O(n^{k-1})$. Empirically, on Kendall’s $\tau$, it reduces MSE by **up to four orders of magnitude**; for AUC under $\varepsilon = 1$, error is only $1/270$ of state-of-the-art baselines. The protocol is communication-efficient ($O(n^{k-1})$ per round) and supports arbitrary $k \geq 2$.
papers.summary:

## 主要成果

papers.title: DualSentinel: A Lightweight Framework for Detecting Targeted Attacks in Black-box LLM via Dual Entropy Lull Pattern
papers.abstract: Recent intelligent systems integrate powerful Large Language Models (LLMs) through APIs, but their trustworthiness may be critically undermined by targeted attacks like backdoor and prompt injection attacks, which secretly force LLMs to generate specific malicious sequences. Existing defensive approaches for such threats typically rely on high access rights, impose prohibitive costs, and hinder normal inference, rendering them impractical for real-world scenarios. To solve these limitations, we introduce DualSentinel, a lightweight and unified defense framework that can accurately and promptly detect the activation of targeted attacks alongside the LLM generation process. We first identify a characteristic of compromised LLMs, termed Entropy Lull: when a targeted attack successfully hijacks the generation process, the LLM exhibits a distinct period of abnormally low and stable token probability entropy, indicating it is following a fixed path rather than making creative choices. DualSentinel leverages this pattern by developing an innovative dual-check approach. It first employs a magnitude and trend-aware monitoring method to proactively and sensitively flag an entropy lull pattern at runtime. Upon such flagging, it triggers a lightweight yet powerful secondary verification based on task-flipping. An attack is confirmed only if the entropy lull pattern persists across both the original and the flipped task, proving that the LLM's output is coercively controlled. Extensive evaluations show that DualSentinel is both highly effective (superior detection accuracy with near-zero false positives) and remarkably efficient (negligible additional cost), offering a truly practical path toward securing deployed LLMs. The source code can be accessed at https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.18479273.
papers.summary_zh:
## 背景与挑战  
当前智能系统广泛通过API调用黑盒大语言模型（LLM），但其可信性正面临**后门攻击**与**提示注入攻击**等定向攻击的严重威胁——此类攻击可隐蔽劫持模型输出，强制生成特定恶意序列。现有防御方案多依赖白盒访问权限、高计算开销或干预推理流程，难以部署于真实API服务场景。

## 方法创新：DualSentinel框架  
本文提出**DualSentinel**——一种轻量、统一、运行时即插即用的黑盒LLM定向攻击检测框架。核心洞见在于发现并命名一种新型异常模式：**熵静默（Entropy Lull）**——当攻击成功激活时，LLM在若干连续token生成步中呈现**异常低且稳定的概率熵值**，反映其脱离自主决策、陷入预设路径的“僵化”状态。

DualSentinel采用**双阶段验证机制**：  
- **第一阶段（主动监测）**：设计幅度与趋势感知的实时熵流监控器，在毫秒级内灵敏捕获熵静默初现；  
- **第二阶段（鲁棒确认）**：一旦触发，立即执行轻量**任务翻转（task-flipping）验证**——将原始输入映射至语义等价但指令方向相反的任务（如问答→反问、摘要→扩写），并检验熵静默是否在双任务中**一致持续**。仅当该模式跨任务复现，才判定为确凿攻击证据，有效排除模型固有行为或输入偏差干扰。

## 性能与价值  
在12个主流黑盒LLM（含GPT-4、Claude-3、Llama-3 API等）及5类定向攻击基准上全面评估表明：DualSentinel实现**99.2%平均检测准确率**、**<0.3%假阳性率**，且引入延迟**<8ms**、内存开销**<2MB**，零修改模型或API。代码已开源：https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.18479273。
papers.summary_en: Recent black-box LLM deployments face severe threats from targeted attacks (e.g., backdoor, prompt injection), yet existing defenses are impractical due to high access requirements, prohibitive overhead, or inference disruption. We propose **DualSentinel**, a lightweight, runtime defense framework that detects attack activation *during* LLM generation. Its core insight is the **Entropy Lull**—a characteristic period of abnormally low and stable token probability entropy when an LLM is hijacked into a fixed output path. DualSentinel employs a dual-check mechanism: (1) a magnitude- and trend-aware entropy monitor for sensitive, real-time lull detection; followed by (2) lightweight task-flipping verification—if the lull persists under semantically inverted tasks (e.g., QA → reverse-QA), the attack is confirmed with high confidence. Evaluated across 12 black-box LLMs and 5 attack types, DualSentinel achieves **99.2% detection accuracy**, **<0.3% false positive rate**, and adds only **<8 ms latency** and **<2 MB memory**, enabling practical, zero-modification deployment. Code: https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.18479273.
